title: Isolate exactly the unit you measure
description: Ensure the benchmark times only the code under test, excluding setup, I/O, and harness overhead that distort microbenchmark results.
body: Use when writing a microbenchmark for a function or hot path. Do not include allocation, fixture setup, or logging inside the timed region, and do not let the compiler optimize the work away. Actions: move setup outside the timed loop, consume the result (e.g. a sink/blackhole) so it is not dead-code-eliminated, and measure harness overhead separately. Validation signals include a stable per-iteration time and a control run of an empty loop to quantify overhead. Risk boundary: a microbenchmark that times setup or gets optimized away produces confident but meaningless numbers; if the measured unit cannot be isolated, prefer a realistic macrobenchmark over a misleading micro one.
